case_title: MEERA BALAKUMAR VS. EMMA FAHY ET AL

REPLY TO PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF MEERA BALAKUMAR'S COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 210051494) FILED BY DEFENDANT FAHY, EMMA VELJANOV, BOBAN

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF MEERA BALAKUMAR'S COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NUISANCE. THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH FACTS (NO CONCLUSIONS) IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE OPPOSITION CONTAINED VERY FEW CASES AND NO CASES AT ALL WITH SIMILAR FACTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER THAT LEAVES SPACE FOR THE COURT TO DECIDE WHETHER A SEPARATE MOTION MUST BE FILED FOR PLAINTIFF TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO INCLUDE ADDITIONAL CAUSES OF ACTION. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART, AND MOOT IN PART. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THIS REQUEST APPEARS TO BE UNOPPOSED. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF MALICE, OPPRESSION OR FRAUD. MOTION IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE REQUEST TO REMOVE THE DEFENDANT FROM THE PREMISES. PLAINTIFF SHALL ALLEGE FACTS AND THE BASIS FOR SUCH REQUEST. THE COURT NOTES AGAIN THAT THERE ARE NO CASES CITED IN OPPOSITION TO SUPPORT THE AVAILABILITY OF SUCH RELIEF UNDER THE LAW. MOTION IS OTHERWISE MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER THAT LEAVES SPACE FOR THE COURT TO DECIDE WHETHER A SEPARATE MOTION MUST BE FILED FOR PLAINTIFF TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO INCLUDE ADDITIONAL CAUSES OF ACTION. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO ALL ALLEGATIONS REGARDING SEPTEMBER 2023 EVENTS. SUCH EVENTS OCCURRED AFTER THE COMPLAINT WAS FILED AND PLAINTIFF SHALL PROPERLY SEEK LEAVE TO FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL COMPLAINT. MOTION IS OTHERWISE DENIED. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR. INSUFFICIENT NOTICE PER CCP 437C(A)(2). THE COURT NOTES THAT THERE IS NO PROOF OF SERVICE ON FILE FOR JULY 16, 2025 HEARING. IF THE MOTION WAS SERVED ON JULY 16, 2025, THE MOTION WOULD BE UNTIMELY. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT ANY CLAIM THAT JULY 14, 2025 VERSION OF THE MOTION (WHICH ATTEMPTED TO SET THE HEARING IN DEPARTMENT 505 FOR AUGUST 12, 2025), ASSUMING IT WAS IN FACT SERVED (NOT CLEAR BASED ON PARAGRAPH 8 OF TOLES DECLARATION FILED ON SEPTEMBER 19, 2025), WOULD HAVE PROVIDED SUFFICIENT NOTICE, IS NOT SUPPORTED BY THE LAW. THE COURT DECLINES MOVING PARTIES' INVITATION TO DISREGARD THE LAW BASED ON UNSUPPORTED BY ANY AUTHORITY ARGUMENT THAT THE RULES REGARDING NOTICE ARE "ZOMBIFIED." JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE DEMURER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE, INVASION OF PRIVACY, INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND NUISANCE. THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUFFICIENTLY STATED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

David Rhouda; 415-518-1683; Rouda Law Firm PC; counsel for plaintiff appearing via Zoom.
Cassady Toles; 628-253-5070; Tyson & Mendes LLP; counsel for defendants, Emma Fahy & Boban Beljanov appearing via Zoom.
Amanda Figueroa; 213-615-7000; Freeman Mathis & Gary, LLP; counsel for defendants, Emma Fahy & Boban Beljanov appearing by court call.

Counsels for defendants contest the courts tentative ruling. Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants' Motion for Summary Judgment is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 437c(a)(2). The Court notes that there is no proof of service on file for July 16, 2025 hearing. If the motion was served on July 16, 2025, the motion would be untimely. The Court further notes that any claim that July 14, 2025 version of the motion (which attempted to set the hearing in department 505 for August 12, 2025), assuming it was in fact served (not clear based on Paragraph 8 of Toles Declaration filed on September 19, 2025), would have provided sufficient notice, is not supported by the law. The Court declines moving parties' invitation to disregard the law based on unsupported by any authority argument that the rules regarding notice are "zombified."

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-01-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT EMMA FAHY, BOBAN VELJANOV MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The demurer is overruled as to the causes of action for negligence, invasion of privacy, Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress and nuisance. These causes of action are sufficiently stated. Demurrer to the cause of action for civil stalking is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of this cause of action.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Harold E. Kahn ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Nov-28-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS EMMA FAHY AND BOBAN VELJANOV'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF MEERA BALAKUMAR'S COMPLAINT

Appearances: S. Michael Lee of Tyson & Mendes LLP (628.253.5070) for defendants and moving parties; Charles Stone of Goethals Legal, PC (650.218.4319) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED in part and overruled in part. Demurer is overruled as to the cause of action for nuisance. This cause of action is sufficiently alleged. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-28-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS EMMA FAHY AND BOBAL VELJANOV'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT

Appearances: S. Michael Lee of Tyson & Mendes LLP (628.253.5070) for defendants and moving parties; Charles Stone of Goethals Legal, PC (650.218.4319) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED in part, and moot in part. Motion is granted as to the request for attorney's fees without leave to amend. This request appears to be unopposed. Motion is granted as to the request for punitive damages with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of malice, oppression or fraud. Motion is granted with leave to amend as to the request to remove the defendant from the premises. Plaintiff shall allege facts and the basis for such request. The Court notes again that there are no cases cited in opposition to support the availability of such relief under the law. Motion is otherwise moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order that leaves space for the Court to decide whether a separate motion must be filed for plaintiff to amend the complaint to include additional causes of action. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)
